{"step_by_step_thinking": "Sulphuric acid is an acid, and acid burns typically cause coagulation necrosis, not blisters. Blisters are more commonly associated with alkali burns. Ulcerated patches can occur in chemical burns, and the absence of singeing of hairs is a characteristic of acid burns since they cause coagulation necrosis and do not liquefy the skin as much as alkali burns do.", "answer_choice": "A"}
